content:
# No Publish


Adding Cache


Ah, GitHub Pages modifies the playing field completely. Because GitHub Pages is purely static hosting, you don’t have a live Node.js/Express server to read or write files on the fly when a user visits.
Furthermore, you cannot safely put your Google API Key directly into frontend JavaScript. If you do, anyone who visits your website can inspect the source code, steal your key, and run up your API quota.
However, you can absolutely do this in JavaScript by combining frontend JS with a clever GitHub Actions trick.
Here is how you handle it safely and statically.
The Strategy: "Build-Time Caching"
Instead of checking the time on the user's browser, you let GitHub Actions do the time-check.
You set a GitHub Action to run every hour. But instead of blindly downloading the file every single time (which wastes GitHub Action minutes), the script checks Google's modifiedTime first. If the file hasn't changed since the last build, it stops immediately.
Step 1: The Node Script (check-and-build.js)
You will keep your Markdown file in your GitHub repo (e.g., content/document.md). This script will run inside GitHub Actions every hour. It fetches the metadata first to see if an update is even necessary.
JavaScript
const fs = require('fs');


const DOCUMENT_ID = process.env.DOCUMENT_ID;
const API_KEY = process.env.GOOGLE_API_KEY;
const TRACKING_FILE = './content/last-modified.txt';
const MD_FILE = './content/document.md';


async function sync() {
  try {
    // 1. Fetch the last modified time from Google Drive API
    const metaUrl = `https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files/${DOCUMENT_ID}?fields=modifiedTime&key=${API_KEY}`;
    const metaRes = await fetch(metaUrl);
    if (!metaRes.ok) throw new Error('Failed to fetch metadata');
    const { modifiedTime } = await metaRes.json();


    // 2. Read the last stored modified time from our repo
    let localModifiedTime = '';
    if (fs.existsSync(TRACKING_FILE)) {
      localModifiedTime = fs.readFileSync(TRACKING_FILE, 'utf-8').trim();
    }


    // 3. If they match, the file hasn't changed. Exit early!
    if (modifiedTime === localModifiedTime) {
      console.log('No changes detected in Google Doc. Skipping update.');
      return;
    }


    // 4. If they don't match, fetch the new content
    console.log('New changes detected! Fetching fresh content...');
    const exportUrl = `https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files/${DOCUMENT_ID}/export?mimeType=text/plain&key=${API_KEY}`;
    const contentRes = await fetch(exportUrl);
    const markdownContent = await contentRes.text();


    // 5. Update both the markdown file and our tracking timestamp file
    fs.writeFileSync(MD_FILE, markdownContent, 'utf-8');
    fs.writeFileSync(TRACKING_FILE, modifiedTime, 'utf-8');
    console.log('Cache successfully updated.');


  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error syncing document:', error);
    process.exit(1);
  }
}


sync();


Step 2: The GitHub Action Workflow
This workflow runs on a schedule. Because of our script above, if there are no changes, it finishes in about 5 seconds and commits nothing, keeping your workflow usage incredibly lightweight.
YAML
name: Hourly Google Doc Sync


on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 * * * *' 
  workflow_dispatch: 
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
      with:
        node-version: '20'


    - name: Check and Update Cache
      env:
        DOCUMENT_ID: ${{ secrets.DOCUMENT_ID }}
        GOOGLE_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.GOOGLE_API_KEY }}
      run: node check-and-build.js


    - name: Commit changes if Google Doc updated
      run: |
        git config --global user.name "github-actions[bot]"
        git config --global user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
        git add content/document.md content/last-modified.txt
        if ! git diff-index --quiet HEAD; then
          git commit -m "chore: update cached google doc content"
          git push
        fi


Step 3: Fetching the MD file on your Frontend Website
Now, your GitHub Pages site hosts a completely static, up-to-date document.md file safely inside your repository. On your actual website (index.html or your frontend JS framework), you just fetch that local file natively.
No API keys exposed to the browser, no lag, and no loading time penalty for your users.
JavaScript
// In your frontend browser JavaScript (e.g., app.js)
async function loadContent() {
  try {
    // Fetch the static markdown file hosted right alongside your site
    const response = await fetch('./content/document.md');
    const markdownText = await response.text();
    
    // Optional: Use a frontend library like 'marked' to convert MD to HTML
    // document.getElementById('content-box').innerHTML = marked.parse(markdownText);
    
    document.getElementById('content-box').innerText = markdownText;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Could not load cached file:", error);
  }
}


loadContent();


Why this is the ultimate GitHub Pages hack:
1. Security: Your Google API Key remains hidden as a GitHub Repository Secret. The user's browser never sees it.
2. Performance: Serving a .md file straight from GitHub Pages CDN takes milliseconds.
3. Efficiency: The hourly action executes, but by comparing timestamps (modifiedTime), it avoids making unnecessary file exports or redundant git commits if no one has touched the Google Doc.




# No Publish


Poets shouldn't drink:


I have no interest in writing poetry, unless I'm drunk apparently. This is a collection of the bad poems I've found the morning after a heavy night.


By Badface


Part of me 
will always be
Living by the sea 


#


Forty, better than I thought it would be
I will definitely admit it's lonely
Really that isn't nearly as scary
Because I finally quite like me


#


She screams at me
And it feels healthy
Seeing her angry
Delights me


She's getting pissed
And slightly dangerous 
Something about this
Fills my senses


#


I tilt my head back and spit at the sun 
Because they told me it couldn't be done
And in truth they were not wrong


But I will always question, 
Any rule or restriction 
Next time I'll have a napkin


#


Making the same mistakes in nicer places
Surrounded by less and less old faces 


#


I killed a guy on a late night walk
His death didn't last long
Just 30 seconds and 30 years
But I'm still living it 
These things happen
But not to other people
They say he deserved it
And I would know he didn't 
Not sure who they're trying to comfort
Serves me right for making it awkward


#


Summer's strangely beautiful 
Sometimes I see it through the sadness
Lying in the park things are looking up
Tears create a kaleidoscope of leaves
Last time I saw something this beautiful 
It ended in the hospital


#


I'm vibing, I'm surviving
Some days I am thriving
I'm a houseplant 
Used to be a wild thing 
I’m fighting to be,
a peace lily


#


I can see the miracle in your mirror 
You weren't created
You had some help
But you built yourself 


#


When I found that folder on your laptop
It was a slow unfolding shock
I realised three months in
You'd planned this whole thing


You'd designed the rollercoaster
Strapped in by a passenger
Too dizzy to understand
Being sick was the whole plan


You'd done such good work on me
If you'd handed me the key
I’d have thrown it in the sea
You made it better than being free


It's hard to explain
Trying sounds insane
I suspect you knew that
I suspect you knew everything 


#


I didn't help build this city
But this city helped build me


#


I'm a juggler juggling jugulars
You ain't never met a thug like us
I'll Leave you the poorest
Discover rock bottom is porous


#


You're not the one that got away
You're the one I got away from


#


Lying in bed and rotting together
Running together 
Lying beside each other
Lying inside each other


You are nothing without me
I'm nothing when I'm with you
I love you. You're incapable of  loving me 
Love doesn't hurt like this 


Taking me to the edge 
You're killing me exquisitely 
Not strong enough to leave you
Only weak enough to breathe you


Given everything to you 
There’s nothing left to take
But I am the user 
Gave it all so I can’t be a loser


Never knew rock bottom
Would be comfort to sit in
Pushing everyone away
And I like it that way


Showing me the simplicity
Of living in a prison at sea
This is so past romantic
Be lucky to land on pathetic


#


Keep it light, keep it breezy
Swallow all the suffering
Even as it's eating me


#


You can see inside of me
But there's no MRI for memory
You can see every part of me
But there's no X-ray for dreams


I don't believe in people's souls
But some of you are full of holes


#


Bought myself a birthday cake
So things are really going great
At this point I'm not certain 
light the candles or the curtain


Life keeps throwing punches
And I take them on the chin
But I'm starting to believe 
I should duck and weave


What doesn't kill me, makes me stronger
But if this goes on much longer
I'll definitely need a guarantee 
That it won't kill me


#


Already found the fountain of youth
Drank that bar dry
Danced on the water table
Snapped the tap
Pissed up the wall while I was still able


#


Never wanted to grow old
I'm nostalgic for when I felt suicidal
You're a half remembered memory
Of who I wanted you to be
I never stopped loving you
But I started loving me too
Finally earned some money
Better than I thought it would be
Reluctantly have to admit
The sacrifices were worth it


#


Do you believe God made you?
Do you believe God made a mistake?
I do not believe God makes mistakes
If God made us, 
They made us strong enough to make ourselves


Do not pray to god for help
Pray to god that you can help yourself
And if you can’t help yourself,
Pray to god that someone they made can help you
And if someone helps you when you cannot help yourself
Pray to god that you remember to help someone else in the future


If you believe god built this country
I say people who believe in god build this county
If you believe god built this church
I say we built this church
We built all the churches that have ever been
And we will build the churches that have not yet been


Because that is how god built us


#


Life is a journey 
And I'm a wanderer


#


I think we could have been happy
If you had just let me
At least I think I would have been


#


Every road leads to our graves
And you’re driving without brakes


#


I got cigarette smoke in my black eye
Wash my wound with whiskey and it made me cry


#


No good men in history 
Only tyrants and misery


#


Wrap myself in a blanket made of memory
And it makes me cold


#


I'll always be there to bite the bad bits off your banana


#


You can overcome every problem, until the last one.


#


It may never be known
How the seed of your suicide was sown 
Did you shed like a sycamore
Spinning crazily to the floor
To the ground, to the earth 
Sprinkling, still birth


#


If you get it 
you don't want it
If you want it
You don't get it


#


Time heals all wounds
Unless I listen to my sad tunes 
Blow my nose on scar tissue
It's true that I miss you


But the you that I miss Is missing
I'm missing true you as I miss you
You've changed into a new you 
And memories of you change too


I've been taken by a mistaken memory
What I remember isn't how it used to be
You used to use and consume me
Now you bruise and confuse me


#


I'm a war with myself 
And I don't know who is winning


#


Reasons to revel in existence
Trees, frisbees and rice crispies
Reasons not to despair
A chair, a spiral stair and dogs with long hair


#


Envy will make a good friend and enemy
Only seeing green when they look at me


#


I’m omnidirectional
Unstoppable
I’ll take everything from you
Everything you are
Everything you build
Tick tock
I won't stop
Tick tock
I grind sand from a rock
I’ve got all the me in the world
To see your life unfurled
I’m hiding in your mirror
I’m in your brick walls
I’m hiding in your liver
I’m in your eyeballs


#


All this productivity is killing me
I just wanna act silly
I'm not talking pizza party
But I gotta earn the boss another Milly 


#


Things haven't gotten worse since you were younger, you have 
Things haven't changed since the good old days, you have 
The world hasn't gotten worse, you have
The universe doesn't have the obligation to fix you, you have 


#


Be the change you want to see in the world
Because the the world really needs you to change 


#


I’m not a man of action
I’m a man of action men
I’m a virtual veteran
Countless battles I’ve spectated in
You call me keyboard warrior
Doesn’t quite convey the horror yeah
You’ll never know how I feel
Some of it almost felt real
I’m the jailer in the panopticon
Always know what’s going on
Always know they’re doing it wrong
Never quite following along
I like my excitement fictional, digital


#


A motivational quote won't change you 
But getting angry at one might 


#


I'm not afraid of dying alone
I'm afraid of so much more than that
I'm not afraid of dying alone
I'm afraid of living long enough to get there


#


Sixteen forty nine
End of a royal time
We cut the head off a king
You can do anything
Charles the first
Need not be the last 
Flowing within me
Is that big axe energy 


#


You say to grow up, stop having fun
So I shoot you with my bubble gun
You think your opinion matters to me
And want me to live deadly seriously
So I put a bubble in your head
Leave you stupid and stupidly dead 


#


Love yourself
Nobody else is going to 


#


I always think it's funny when racist people in England are like 'we used to leave our doors unlocked and everyone knew each other!' 


Because I live in a majority muslim area and they don't lock their doors and we all know each other.


The England you 'miss' actually still exists, you're just scared of it and would never visit.


It’s almost like England didn't start locking its doors and stop talking to neighbours, you did.


#


You always made me cry
But it used to be with laughter
Except sometimes the morning after
You always kept it light
After a heavy night
I never knew your fight


Deep down I always knew 
That only you could kill you
But we all said we all wanted to
Thought you knew it wasn't true
I watched everything try 
and still you wouldn't die
You weren't the only one who wanted you alive 
But it was you who you couldn't survive


#


From port st to peterloo
There's such a lot to do
When I'm drinking in you
Never felt more welcome
Than a hug from a hell spawn 
The devil's in the detail
Six floors of retail
A tigers den of scary men
Satan's slaves on Salisbury plain
Drinking in a toilet again
A palace made of lace
A castle for a rascal
Say yes to the bar
That loves you how you are
Ride a bay horse
Through a marble arch
Tie yourself in knots
Trying to empty all these pots


#


You need to understand 
that the past is quicksand
Start living in the present
Enjoy the surface tension


#


When they promise that they can change, you should always believe them. But you should also know that they're going to get worse.


#


It’s us versus the world, and the world is going to win
But that doesn’t mean we can’t dance and sing


#


Nobody talks about what to do when you catch your dreams. Can it still be fun when the chase is done. Or is it time to dream another one.
What if you wake up when you catch up. At heart we're hunters, we love the kill. 


#


I wish my friends wouldn't kill themselves
I find it's bad for my mental health


#


I've got nowhere to be
And nothing to do
So I might as well be with you
I've got nowhere to be
And nothing to do
So I might as well do it with you


You're a drug
You're a hug
An embrace
Around the waste


#


Decided to start enjoying my life
Already tried everything else
And none of it worked
Hit rock bottom
More times than Dwayne Johnson’s toilet seat
Run out of bad options
The only thing left for me
Is just being happy
Staring at a sunset
Trying to understand it
Tell myself it’s beautiful
And it makes my life feel full
Gonna fake it till I make it
Had enough of breaking it


#


The right wing don’t hate you.


It’s worse than that. 


They don’t even think about you at all.


They can’t even comprehend the pain they cause. It’s not even their fault, our lives are just so far from their lived experience. The truth is almost everyone of any party who makes it into politics is unlikely to have faced true hopelessness even for a few weeks.


We judge a country on GDP, inflation, employment and the price of petrol. What does any of that really mean? Those easy numbers bundle so many complex factors together that the whole becomes meaningless. It’s like judging how beautiful a garden is by how much it weighs.


I want a world where we judge a country on the number of children who will be lucky to get dinner, let alone a Christmas dinner this year. Or the number of pensioners who’ll wet themselves to avoid moving from the warmth they’ve created under every blanket they own this winter. 


Those examples are extreme, but it doesn’t make them any less true.


But how about something more relatable, like the number of people who help a neighbour or stranger without expectation of reward or return. Or the number of people who feel confident about their children’s ability to buy a home and live there without fear.


Imagine a government held to account under those metrics, imagine a country that forced a government to make every decision after at least considering how it will affect the people they can never really understand - but should still care about.


The true joke is, even if we do decide to keep judging a country on GDP, inflation, employment and the price of petrol, the Tories have failed us utterly.


#


Grief is harder on a hangover
I got a cemetery of regrets 
I miss all my dead friends
I want to hear them again
But not enough to join them 


#


You got me
Thigh slappin
Foot tappin’
Belly laughin


You got me 
Boot kickin'
Lip lickin’
Whiskey sippin’


You got me 
Head shakin’
Shot taking
Heart racing


I wanna hold you close
But I let you roam far
You lift the roof off this bar
There ain't a single person in here
Who don't wanna know who you are


#


To die is to live 
and to live is to die
But we wouldn't be human
If we didn't ask why
We know we can't stop it
Still we can't help but try


#


I built my life around you
Feels like a prison, what can I do
You know I’m never gone
Living a panopticon


#


You can tell me what to do
Because I don't know what to do
You can't tell me what to think
Because I don't know how to think


#


Irritants gonna irritate
Wankers gonna masturbate
But the great stay great
A mosquito don't know
How to not mosquito though


#


Everyone’s the best at something
Can’t promise it’s not a dumb thing
Take this guy
He can tie a perfect bowtie
Then there’s this girl
She can do a perfect curl
And this dude here has perfectly symmetrical 
testicles
It’s actually caused by something medical
That is tragically terminal
He’s going to live a short life
But what a short life!
Shine bright you crazy diamond
Can’t even show a doctor as he’d blind them
Besides there is no surgery
To make your balls more wonky
Why would someone invent that


#


Everytime you do something beautiful
It makes you more beautiful
Doesn't matter what you do
As long as it's not for you


I don't have to see you do it
We'll be able to see you've done it


#


Money makes the world go round
And it sure spins fast when were out on the town


#


Yes you're weak and you're weird
You are different that is clear
But it's up to you
What that means to you


#


We strangle the things we love
Because we just can't get enough
I make you into my womb
As I kill you in my living room


# No Publish


A ya mammas so fat joke
The closest she's come to healthy eating is putting a condom on a cucumber


I know what you're thinking, why has she put a condom on it?


Because when she's finished she's serving you salad for tea.


#


Drunken sailor extra verses:


Put him in the Brigg with a greasy pig
Serve his breakfast in the crows nest
Have him scale to to the top sail
Hide a shamrock in his hammock
Make him jump from his top bunk


#


Wasn't built to last
Then I met you and rebuilt fast
Couldn't live for myself
Until I'd die for someone else


#


There's nobility 
In futility 
When every life
Has dignity


#


You get all the money
You get all the love
You get all the things
I've ever dreamed of
You won't talk to me
Because I'm not pretty
This is a beautocracy


#


Life is pointless
But this meant something


#


I'm a mess
Couldn't care less
Fuck the rest
I'm the best


#


I'm making memories
Not enemies
You'll only see
The best of me
You'll never see
The rest of me
At the pub I'll gettem in
Leave you feeling like ketamin
Wake up regretting it
But never quite forgetting it
One life, you're living it
This is it, might as well enjoy it


#


Children's book full of mirrors:


There's another baby in my house


You know what I noticed the other day?
There's a baby that lives in the hallway
I also see them in the tv when it's turned off
I saw them in something we were putting in the loft
They must have made it out again because soon.. 
I saw them in the bathroom!
Mum opened up a little square (make up mirror)
And I even saw the baby there
I'm not sure what to think
I even saw them in a sink
I think they might be everywhere I look
Because I even saw them in this book!


#


If I ever loved you
I love you forever
Maybe not completely
But part of me, definitely
not a big part of me, maybe
not the smartest part of me, certainly
But if I don't love you forever
Then I never loved you at all


#


We're protesting peacefully 
Come join the right side of history
We're protesting peacefully 
Why don't you come and talk to me


#


Spent our whole life trying to talk
Talk you down from bad decisions
While you talked us up onto them
The fall always seemed so small
Never knew the stakes were so high
Nobody does until the day they die


#


Read your history
Google guillotine
The poor have done it before


Palisade a barricade
We can do it again


#


There once was a giraffe
With a tremendous laugh
It would start in their peck
And wobble up their neck
Then burst off their tongue
It was brilliant fun for everyone


#


There once was a mighty dinosaur
And you won't believe the things they heard and saw
Once they heard a mighty roar
They looked under a bush, what do you think they saw?


They saw a teeny tiny mouse 
Stood beside a teeny tiny house
The roar had come from the mouse's mouth
Because someone had squashed the mouse's house


The dinosaur saw they'd heard the mouses roar 
because they'd stomped the mouse's house into the floor
The mouse looked up and said, "good job I was in my garden"
"Else I'd have been squished flat too then"


The dinosaur felt quite guilty
So they said to the mouse right quickly
"I'm sorry for what I did
My feet are just so big”


“But my arms are very small
So it will take no time at all
To build you a house on the floor
Even better than before"


And that's exactly what they did
The new house was strong and big
So the mouse and the dinosaur
Both liked what they heard and saw


#


I'm tom tailor the animal tailor
If you're confused, let me regale ya
When an animal needs clothes
My shop is where it goes


I was asked to knit a scarf
For a very tall giraffe
I said, “You're having a laugh!”
“That would take month and a half”


What can you expect
With such a long neck?
I couldn't do it alone if you paid me
So I found a little old lady


And put her and her mates in a row
Knitting together, elbow to elbow
Suddenly, it wasn't so slow
It still took us a week though


After that I made a hat 
for a hairless cat 
it didn't take long 
except when we put it on


Then I made a waterproof coat 
for a goat who lived on a boat 
It had to look incredible 
but be completely inedible


Then I worked for a kangaroo 
who wanted a jumper and shoes too
they said thank you
Now its as warm as Australia in the zoo


I made a big bow tie
For a happy hippo guy
It was so wide
We had to make it outside


I made something quite funny 
for a bobtail bunny 
it was a belt for their money 
that wrapped around their tummy


I met a smart monkey 
who politely asked me 
for a top hat and tails
so they could throw poop at the Prince of Wales


There was a furry anteater
Who said 'nice to meetya'
I think i've chosen some lederhosen
I can fit my nose in


Then I met a swarm of bees
Who said they struggle with sleeves
So they were very pleased
When I made some stripy dungarees


#


Bingle bongle
Jingle jongle
Wimble womble
Ooof!
That's a morris dancer
Who didn't take care
And fell down the stairs


#


I once knew a great big hippo
Whose voice was very low
And anywhere they would go
People would ask the hippo,
“Why is your voice so low?”
Hippo would say, “I don't know”
But it gets more and more low
The bigger and bigger I GROW
Except when I breathe helium
My voice goes all squeally then!


#


There once was a little kitten
Who slept in a child’s mitten
But they grew too big to fit in
So they found baby bed to sit in


#


There was a little boy called Simon
He said, “I think I can fly mum”
She said, “flying would be fun”
“But unfortunately it can't be done”
He looked sad and said, “okay.”
Then jumped up and flew away


#


There once was a round little puppy 
Who loved to get muddy and mucky
Everyone thought they were yucky
But in fact they were very lucky
Because they loved to have fun
And didn’t care how they looked when done


#


I met a little cat called Marmalade
Who hated to be in the shade
They’d find where it was sunny
And roll over to warm their tummy


#


Once in the park I met a little green alien
They saw a swing and asked me to explain it to them
They asked, “do you sing on a swing?” 
“Is that the done thing?”


I said no, 
You don’t sing on a swing,
You swing on a swing, 
That's the done thing.


They asked me but can you sing on a swing?
Or is that not the done thing?
I said it’s not the done thing,
To sing on a swing,


But here’s the thing,
It does sound fun to sing on a swing
And now I want to sing on a swing
Even if it’s not the done thing to sing on a swing


So me and the alien, 
jumped on two swings again, 
And sung and swung, 
And it was great, great fun


#


There was a little girl called Simone 
Who tripped and fell in her phone
She felt all flat and alone
The screen wouldn’t flex or bend
So she typed a message to a friend
Attached herself and pressed send


#


When I lost you I finally understood why people write love songs. From the Greeks to the greats. Every word penned made sense to me.


It's so easy to write about love. We've been practicing for countless generations. All the similies and metaphors have been used before. Codified and defined. A box of Chocolates and a bouquet of roses. Butterflies and bottomless eyes.


Nobody has done the other end. The very end and everything after. We're struck speechless in the face of true eternity. Words fail us, heads empty. Unless you want empty platitudes and the same old rehearsed verses.


It's too big, too painful and too permanent.


People say love lasts forever, that's just a distraction from our true destination.

# No Publish


Sexual Assault Story


Watching I May Destroy You on iPlayer like many others I was struck by the fantastically nuanced depictions of sexual assault. This made me reflect differently on two experiences I had at University and realise that they were probably sexual assault. I was sexually assaulted. Now the fact that it took me ten years to realise this, probably suggests these events weren’t too traumatic. Also that it can be very hard for men to accept themselves as the victim. But it is also more complicated than that, I knew both women incredibly well and hold absolutely no anger towards them and completely understand their motivations and don’t believe they were trying to do any harm - and arguably they didn’t thanks in part to all of our obliviousness too what we were doing. This is what makes sexual assault so complicated. I am also aware that my experiences don’t really feel like a ‘proper’ sexual assault, I’m partially writing this to explore my feelings and another confusing aspect of consent, assault and the messy grey areas. I also have a vague notion of writing this and maybe showing it to one of the women, who I still have an email address and getting her, no doubt very different, side of the story. Not least because I only experienced the event from one perspective, but also memory is far from perfect and the passage of years has probably warped and changed the event in subtle and dramatic ways for both of us. That said, an email from an ex-partner you haven’t spoken to in five years asking ‘do you think we assaulted each other almost 15 years ago’ probably wouldn’t be a very nice thing to do to someone.
